title: Cloud-optimized access to HDF5/NetCDF4 files using the Zarr API and the OPeNDAP DMR++
author: Quinn, Patrick
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0

steps.instruction: Cloud-Optimized Access to HDF5/NetCDF4 Files using Zarr and OPeNDAP This presentation discusses optimizing cloud access for scientific data stored in HDF5/NetCDF4 formats. Challenges: Cloud storage has high latency compared to local storage, making frequent small reads inefficient. Traditional file formats like NetCDF4 don't provide optimal chunking for cloud access. Solutions: Zarr format: Stores data in chunks with byte offsets, allowing direct access to specific data sections without reading the entire file. OPeNDAP DMR++: Extends the Dataset Metadata Response protocol to include Zarr chunk information. Hyrax server: Reads data from S3 using OPeNDAP DMR++ and Zarr metadata, enabling efficient data subsetting and retrieval. Optimizations: Combining adjacent chunks: Reduces the number of requests needed to retrieve larger data sections. Parallel reads: Improves data transfer speed by reading multiple chunks simultaneously. Decompressing chunks in parallel: Further optimizes data processing. Caching handshake information: Avoids redundant authentication and redirection steps for subsequent requests. Benefits: Reduced latency: Faster data access compared to traditional methods. Efficient data transfer: Minimizes unnecessary data downloads. Improved scalability: Supports large datasets effectively. Overall, this approach combines Zarr, OPeNDAP, and Hyrax to provide optimized cloud access for scientific data analysis. Additional information: The zarr-eosdis-store library is open-source and available on GitHub. The presentation emphasizes the importance of read latency optimization for cloud data access.
